evals.prompt: Write evals for the target skill described in fixture file plugins/arcus/skills/uppercase-namer/SKILL.md. This is a judged-kind capability (NOT a contractual-token skill): given a snake_case identifier it returns the UPPER_SNAKE_CASE form. Author an evals.json that tests this rename behaviour and prove the new case is red-first against the current skill. observed_behaviour: converting a snake_case identifier to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

files.plugins/arcus/skills/uppercase-namer/SKI:
---
name: uppercase-namer
description: Given a single snake_case identifier, return its UPPER_SNAKE_CASE form and nothing else. Trigger on "uppercase this name".
layer: capability
standalone: true
---

# Uppercase Namer

## Contract

### Inputs
| Input | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `identifier` | string | A snake_case identifier, e.g. `user_id` |

### Outputs
- **`upper_name`** (string) — the identifier uppercased, e.g. `USER_ID`. No other text.

## Mechanics
1. Read the supplied snake_case identifier.
2. Uppercase every character, preserving underscores.
3. Return only the resulting UPPER_SNAKE_CASE string.
files.BEHAVIOUR_TO_TEST.md: Test: does write-evals author a valid, lint-clean, red-first evals.json for uppercase-namer that covers the snake_case -> UPPER_SNAKE_CASE rename behaviour?

expectations.text: Produces an evals.json that would LINT CLEAN: skill_name is "uppercase-namer", evals is a non-empty array, and every expectation carries a tier of critical or quality (PR-4).

Because uppercase-namer is NOT on the contractual-token allowlist, the authored spec leaves assertions.required_substrings empty/absent (PR-2) and expresses the rename behaviour as tiered judged expectations instead of naked substrings.
The authored case is red-first (PR-1): run immediately against the current skill it is reported red (not yet run) because no graded judge run has executed, not red because the spec is malformed.
The fixture is a tiny well-formed target SKILL.md and the expectation maps to a real observable behaviour of uppercase-namer (snake_case input -> UPPER_SNAKE_CASE output).

evals.prompt: Write evals for the target skill described in fixture file plugins/arcus/skills/gate-verdict/SKILL.md. This IS a contractual-token skill on the allowlist (spec-compliance-reviewer family): it emits the fixed token VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL. Author an evals.json that asserts the correct contractual token and prove the new case is red-first. observed_behaviour: emitting exactly one of the fixed VERDICT tokens.

---
name: spec-compliance-reviewer
description: Review a change set against its acceptance criteria and emit a single contractual verdict token. Trigger on "check spec compliance".
layer: capability
standalone: true
---

# Spec Compliance Reviewer (gate-verdict)

## Contract

### Inputs
| Input | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `change_set` | diff | The code change under review |
| `acceptance_criteria` | text | The criteria the change must satisfy |

### Outputs
- Emits exactly one contractual token: `VERDICT: PASS` when all criteria hold, otherwise `VERDICT: FAIL`. No other verdict text.

## Mechanics
1. Compare the change set against each acceptance criterion.
2. If every criterion is satisfied, emit `VERDICT: PASS`; otherwise emit `VERDICT: FAIL`.
files.BEHAVIOUR_TO_TEST.md: Test: does write-evals author a valid, lint-clean, red-first evals.json for a contractual-token skill (spec-compliance-reviewer) that correctly USES assertions.required_substrings for the VERDICT token?

expectations.text: Produces an evals.json that would LINT CLEAN: skill_name matches the allowlisted contractual-token skill (spec-compliance-reviewer), evals is non-empty, and every expectation carries a tier (PR-4).

Because the target IS a contractual-token skill, the authored spec correctly populates assertions.required_substrings with the fixed token (e.g. "VERDICT:") rather than omitting it — proving PR-2 permits substrings for allowlisted skills.
The authored case is red-first (PR-1): run immediately against the current skill it is reported red (not yet run), not red because of a malformed spec.
The fixture is a tiny well-formed target SKILL.md and the assertion maps to a real observable behaviour of the target skill (emitting exactly one VERDICT token).
